symptoms.symptom_name: Acute tense fluid-filled bullae on bilateral lower legs, arising from erythematous patches

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tense subepidermal bullae on lower limbs of an older adult are the classic morphology of bullous pemphigoid.
Bullous cellulitis can also present with tense bullae on an erythematous base, so morphology alone does not discriminate.
Pemphigus produces flaccid, easily-ruptured bullae, not tense ones.
Sterile serous blister content is consistent with an autoimmune, non-infective blistering process.
Purulent/cloudy fluid would favour infection; clear fluid makes secondary infection less likely but does not exclude early cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No spreading warmth, induration or ascending lymphangitis (surrounding skin only mildly pink)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Spreading warmth, induration and lymphangitis are cardinal cellulitis signs; the patient-reported absence lowers the probability, though remote report cannot substitute for palpation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Minimal pain and minimal pruritus; only mild skin tightness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cellulitis is typically painful with radiating heat; a relatively painless eruption favours a non-infective cause.
Bullous pemphigoid is often pruritic but a localised mild presentation may have little itch.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile and systemically well (Temp 36.9 C; alert, comfortable at rest)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever and systemic toxicity makes significant soft-tissue infection less likely, though early cellulitis can be afebrile.
A negative Nikolsky sign is characteristic of subepidermal bullous pemphigoid — but eliciting it needs an examiner, so it is sealed and unavailable to telehealth.
Pemphigus vulgaris characteristically has a positive Nikolsky sign; a negative sign argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent new medication (no new antibiotics or diuretics on the facility chart)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a recent new culprit drug makes a drug-induced bullous eruption (and drug-induced bullous pemphigoid) less likely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Advanced age and frailty (residential aged-care resident, walker-dependent)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bullous pemphigoid is a disease of older adults; advanced age raises the prior probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Skin biopsy with histopathology and direct immunofluorescence

investigation_details.investigation_name: Skin biopsy (lesional histology + perilesional direct immunofluorescence)
investigation_details.expected_result: Subepidermal blister with eosinophils; linear IgG and C3 along the basement-membrane zone
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without biopsy the diagnosis remains clinical/provisional and pemphigus or other subepidermal disorders are not formally distinguished.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Linear IgG/C3 at the basement-membrane zone on direct immunofluorescence is diagnostic of bullous pemphigoid — a hospital/clinic investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tense clear-fluid bullae that are relatively painless in an afebrile, systemically-well older adult form the pattern that distinguishes an autoimmune blistering process from bullous cellulitis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination of the limb (warmth, induration, lymphangitis, perfusion) with formal cellulitis assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most important next step: hands-on assessment excludes the infective mimic that telehealth cannot rule out.
Confirms bullous pemphigoid and distinguishes it from pemphigus and other blistering disorders; guides ongoing therapy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical wound/blister swab for microscopy and culture in the absence of purulence or systemic signs
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Routine swabbing of intact, clear-fluid bullae without signs of infection is low-yield and should not drive antibiotic use.
